name: Cima del Montgó — circular por las rutas VERDE, NARANJA y AZUL
name_original: Cima del Montgó — circular por las rutas verde, naranja y azul
region_intro: Hiking in El Montgó offers urban trails through a distinctive limestone massif that rises dramatically from the Mediterranean coast. The region is characterized by craggy cliffs, diverse Mediterranean scrubland, and regenerating pine forests. Its proximity to the sea allows for unique coastal paths and views where the mountain meets the water. The area encompasses a variety of habitats, providing a rich natural backdrop for urban exploration. Best urban hiking trails in El Montgó The most popular urban hiking route is Hermitage of Father Pere loop from Ermita de Sant Joan, a 2.4 miles (3.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 6 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the area around a historic hermitage. Another top favourite among local hikers is Las Rotas Beach – Marineta Casiana Beach loop from Parc Natural del Montgó, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This route offers coastal scenery connecting two beaches. Local hikers also love the Hermitage of Father Pere – Las Rotas Beach loop from Estatua del Pare Pere, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through coastal and urban landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Urban hiking trails in El Montgó are defined by coastal paths, views of the limestone massif, and Mediterranean vegetation, with options available for different ability levels. The routes in El Montgó are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,200 reviews. More than 6,000 hikers have used komoot to explore El Montgó's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many urban hiking trails are available in El Montgó?
A: El Montgó offers a wide selection of urban hiking trails, with over 45 routes available. These trails cater to various fitness levels, from easy strolls to more moderate excursions, allowing you to explore the diverse landscapes between Denia and Jávea.

Q: Are there easy urban hikes suitable for beginners in El Montgó?
A: Yes, El Montgó has many easy urban hikes perfect for beginners, with over 30 routes classified as easy. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10353121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Father Pere loop from Ermita de Sant Joan&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a pleasant walk with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on urban hikes in El Montgó?
A: Urban hikes in El Montgó provide stunning panoramic views. You can expect breathtaking vistas of the Costa Blanca coastline, the Mediterranean Sea, and the patchwork of orange groves. On clear days, you might even catch a glimpse of the island of Ibiza from higher points. Many trails offer unique perspectives where the dramatic limestone massif meets the sea.

Q: Are there any family-friendly urban walks in El Montgó?
A: Many urban trails in El Montgó are suitable for families. Routes that stick closer to the coast or lower elevations tend to be less strenuous. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33752407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Rotas Beach – Marina at Sunset loop from Bosc de Diana&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option that offers scenic coastal views and is generally flat, making it enjoyable for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on urban hiking trails in El Montgó?
A: Yes, many urban hiking trails in El Montgó are dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural park areas, to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Be sure to carry enough water for both yourself and your pet, particularly during warmer months.

Q: Are there circular urban hiking routes in El Montgó?
A: Yes, El Montgó features several excellent circular urban hiking routes. These are ideal for experiencing varied scenery without retracing your steps. For a moderate coastal experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21937175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Rotas Beach – Marineta Casiana Beach loop from Parc Natural del Montgó&lt;/a&gt;, which combines beachside paths with natural park views.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the urban hiking trails?
A: Urban hikes in El Montgó often lead to fascinating landmarks. You can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/203776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of Jávea&lt;/a&gt;, offering insights into the region's past. Coastal trails might bring you to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/416894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre del Gerro&lt;/a&gt; or the unique sea cave, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada (Cut Cave)&lt;/a&gt;, which is partially man-made. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/461688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cap de Sant Antoni Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; also offers spectacular views.

Q: What is the best time of year for urban hikes in El Montgó?
A: The best time for urban hikes in El Montgó is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the natural landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, so if hiking then, it's best to go early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid the midday sun. Winters are generally mild and pleasant for hiking.

Q: Are there urban trails that offer a mix of coastal and natural park scenery?
A: Absolutely. El Montgó's unique position where the mountain meets the sea allows for trails that seamlessly blend coastal beauty with the rich biodiversity of the natural park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31466942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Father Pere – Las Rotas Beach loop from Estatua del Pare Pere&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering both sea views and glimpses into the Mediterranean scrubland.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about urban hiking in El Montgó?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.45 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning coastal and panoramic views, the diverse landscapes ranging from beaches to rugged cliffs, and the well-marked trails that cater to various abilities. The opportunity to combine natural beauty with historical landmarks is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Is public transport available to access urban hiking trailheads in El Montgó?
A: Public transport options, primarily local bus services, are available in Denia and Jávea, which can provide access to some of the urban trailheads. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and routes in advance, as services might be less frequent to more remote starting points. Many urban trails are accessible directly from the towns themselves.

Q: Where can I find parking for urban hiking trails in El Montgó?
A: Parking is generally available near popular urban trailheads, especially in and around the towns of Denia and Jávea. Look for designated parking areas near beaches, marinas, or specific attractions like the Hermitage of Father Pere. During peak season, parking can fill up quickly, so arriving early is recommended.

Q: Are there any short, easy urban walks suitable for a quick outing?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a shorter, easy urban walk, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33751097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bous a la Mar Sculpture – Port of Dénia loop from Dénia&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This route is relatively flat and takes you along the Dénia waterfront, offering pleasant views and a relaxed atmosphere perfect for a quick stroll.
tips: The ascent is a bit difficult, but it's worth it!
Here you have a beautiful panoramic view of the bay of Dénia on the way up to Mount Penya de l'Àguila, 485 meters high.

short video about my hike in this area could be found under the following link:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1NPr39m3h2g

The top of the Montgó offers unique and amazing views of the coast of Denia and Jávea. You get there along a path that in sections can be somewhat demanding due to the slopes but that is very enjoyable at every step. I recommend going around it and returning along the Camí de Colonia del Montogó, which runs along its entire length facing the sea, a wonderful walk!
The little detour down is worth it. Recommended lamp.
The Camí de la Colonia offers beautiful panoramic views of the city of Dénia, its castle, the Segaria mountain range and the coast of Valencia.

waypoints.name: Camí de la Colònia (Panoramic Trail Overlooking Dénia)
